nodes.description: AQP4-IgG binding activates the classical complement cascade, leading to membrane attack complex formation and astrocyte lysis. This distinguishes NMOSD from MS where complement activation is less prominent.

evidence.reference_title: Eculizumab in Aquaporin-4-Positive Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: At least two thirds of cases are associated with aquaporin-4 antibodies (AQP4-IgG) and complement-mediated damage to the central nervous system.

nodes.description: Long-acting complement C5 inhibitor used for relapse prevention in AQP4-IgG-positive NMOSD.

evidence.reference_title: P.011 Efficacy and safety of ravulizumab in adults with AQP4+ NMOSD: interim analysis from the ongoing phase 3 CHAMPION-NMOSD trial
evidence.snippet: Ravulizumab demonstrated long-term clinical benefit in the prevention of relapses in AQP4+ NMOSD with a safety profile consistent with prior analyses.
